text.title: Churn Basics Core Rules
text.summary: 6 testable rules every application of `Churn Basics` must enforce. Each rule pairs a binary check with a sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Churn report MUST simultaneously include customer churn rate, MRR churn rate, and Net Revenue Retention (NRR). Reporting any one metric in isolation is rejected at review.
rule.rationale: Single-metric churn reports hide expansion vs. contraction dynamics; NRR can mask high logo churn behind upsells. Three metrics together prevent narrative cherry-picking.

rule.statement: Voluntary churn (active cancel) and involuntary churn (failed payment) MUST be tracked separately. Combined numbers conflate marketing failure with billing failure.
rule.rationale: Involuntary churn is a tooling / dunning problem (Recover, Stripe Smart Retries). Voluntary churn is a product / pricing / segment problem. Mixing them sends the team chasing the wrong fix.

rule.statement: Monthly churn MUST be reported as monthly. Annualizing by multiplying by 12 is forbidden — compute compounded annual churn = 1 − (1 − monthly)^12.
rule.rationale: At 5% monthly, true annual churn is 46%, not 60%. Linear annualization overstates the problem and distorts forecasts, board narratives, and runway projections.

rule.statement: Churn MUST be segmented by acquisition cohort AND plan tier. Aggregate-only churn rates are forbidden in board / quarterly reports.
rule.rationale: Aggregate churn averages aging cohorts with fresh ones and free-tier with enterprise. Cohort × plan grids surface the real failure mode — e.g. month-2 churn on starter plan.

rule.statement: Health-score must include >= 3 leading indicators (login frequency drop, feature usage delta, support frustration score). Backtest thresholds on historic churners before going live.
rule.rationale: Lagging churn rate is too late — the customer has decided. Leading indicators give a 2-4 week intervention window. Without backtesting, false-positive rate is high and CS teams ignore alerts.

rule.statement: When preconditions in Applies If fail OR any item in Skip If triggers, do not run this methodology. Document the skip rationale in the artefact's parent log.
rule.rationale: Misapplied methodologies are worse than no methodology — they consume review cycles and produce false confidence.
